question.text: Which regulatory body in India has primary jurisdiction over exchange-traded derivatives?

answer.text: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) regulates all exchange-traded derivatives, with RBI having additional oversight for currency and interest rate derivatives
RBI (Reserve Bank of India) regulates all derivatives including exchange-traded
The Ministry of Finance directly regulates all derivative markets
Each stock exchange independently regulates its own derivative products without any external oversight

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI is the primary regulator for all exchange-traded derivatives under SCRA. However, RBI retains oversight for currency derivatives (underlying forex market regulation) and IR derivatives (underlying bond/interest rate market regulation). This creates a dual regulatory structure for some products. **The Trap:** RBI doesn't regulate equity derivatives. The Ministry of Finance sets policy but doesn't directly regulate. Exchanges are self-regulatory organizations under SEBI oversight. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch8 covers SEBI's role for equity derivatives. Series I Ch8 covers SEBI-RBI dual regulation for currency derivatives. Series IV Ch8 for IR derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding regulatory jurisdiction is practical — product approvals, position limit changes, and margin rule modifications come from different regulators depending on the derivative type.
